segments.title: Commonwealth Invincibles need a carrier-aviation case
segments.summary: The Patreon live frames an eight-ship, 30,000-ton Invincible program around Britain, Australia, Canada, and the political case for keeping carrier aviation.
segments.body: Australia and Canada would need to justify carrier aviation after their historical exits, while a larger Invincible path is tied to Sea Harrier development and the flight-deck implications of a supersonic Harrier route.

evidence.note: The transcript compares the end of Australian and Canadian carrier aviation and explains the strategic and political arguments needed to keep the capability.
The Sea Harrier discussion explains why a supersonic Harrier path could justify a larger Invincible-class flight deck.
